abstract: This paper investigates whether alternative social media platforms contribute to discursive polarization. Published in Comun. Politica in 2025 by Jakob Bæk Kristensen, it examines the relationship between platform choice and polarized discourse. The work situates alternative platforms within broader debates about online political communication and fragmentation of the public sphere.
key_claims: Alternative platforms may play a role in shaping discursive polarization
Platform architecture and user composition are relevant factors in understanding polarized discourse online
methods: Not specified in the available abstract
findings: Not specified in the available abstract
contributions: Contributes to the literature on platform polarization by focusing specifically on alternative (non-mainstream) social media platforms
Frames a research question about whether platform migration drives discursive polarization
framing: The paper is situated within political communication research on polarization, platformization, and the fragmentation of online public discourse, engaging with debates over whether alternative platforms exacerbate ideological divisions.
